title: Design System: Nihar Domala — AI Systems Portfolio

display.purpose: Hero name only, set massive and tight. Appears once.

title.purpose: Project and role titles, terminal chip headings.

body.purpose: Problem/solution sentences and prose, max 70ch, Instrument White.

label.purpose: Machine voice: metrics, badges, terminal chips, form fields, status line.

shadows.name: node-glow-live
shadows.purpose: Soft halo under an active/firing canvas node and the output node. Canvas-rendered, never on DOM chrome.

shadows.name: node-glow-infra
shadows.purpose: Cobalt halo along active synapses and structural network highlights.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default exponential ease-out for state transitions, fades, and tooltips.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Lead action: case-study live demo, contact submit. Goes lime on hover (its one live-signal moment).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #EDEFF2; color: #05070C; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono','Fira Code',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; padding: 12px 28px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #CCFF00; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #CCFF00; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action: GitHub / code link. Hairline border lifts to cobalt on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: #0B0F17; color: #EDEFF2; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono','Fira Code',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; padding: 12px 28px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #3B82F6; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3B82F6; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Tech / Layer Chip
components.description: Small terminal token for tech badges and the glassmorphic layer-hover label.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; background: #0B0F17; color: #3B82F6; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono','Fira Code',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; padding: 4px 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius: 4px; }

components.name: Terminal Input
components.description: CLI-styled contact field with a prompt glyph; border + underglow shift to cobalt on focus.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #0B0F17; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius: 4px; padding: 12px 14px; transition: border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-prompt { color: #3B82F6; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,monospace; } .ds-input { flex: 1; background: transparent; border: none; outline: none; color: #EDEFF2; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono','Fira Code',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9CA3AF; } .ds-field:focus-within { border-color: #3B82F6; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #3B82F6, 0 0 18px rgba(59,130,246,0.25); }

components.name: Case Study Card
components.description: TL;DR project panel: problem/solution neutral, the single impact metric in lime. Flat, hairline border, no shadow, no side-stripe.
components.html:
Real-time NLP Pipeline

Batch ETL could not keep up with streaming inference demand.

Processed 10M+ rows daily · latency -40%
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0B0F17; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius: 14px; padding: 28px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: Geist,system-ui,sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.25rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #EDEFF2; margin: 0 0 12px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Geist,system-ui,sans-serif; font-size: 1.0625rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #EDEFF2; margin: 0 0 16px; max-width: 70ch; } .ds-metric { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono','Fira Code',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #CCFF00; margin: 0; }

narrative.overview: The page is not a portfolio about a neural network; it is one, mid-inference. Data enters at the top (passions), flows down through engineering and ML-stack layers, and converges at the bottom on a single output node whose status reads 'ready.' The aesthetic is a high-end technical instrument, precise and calm, with one live signal that pulses. Density is low by deliberate intent: restraint is the entire argument. Everything structural is rendered in cool infrastructural cobalt; everything alive is rendered in a single electric lime that, by rule, is rare.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black charcoal canvas (#05070C), drenched and absolute.
Two-color logic: cobalt = infrastructure, lime = life. Nothing else competes.
Monospace for machine-spoken strings; Inter for human-spoken text.
One signature interaction (the autonomous canvas pipeline) instead of many small ones.
Performance is visual integrity: a stutter breaks the illusion that the machine is real.

rules.name: The One Live Signal Rule
rules.body: Lime means exactly one thing: active right now. Hovered, firing, the single headline metric, or the one moment asking to be clicked. Cobalt carries everything merely structural.

rules.name: The Tone-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth on this dark surface comes from lifting tone (#05070C to #0B0F17), not from drop shadows. Glows are the only exception and belong to the network, not UI chrome.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Inter is the human voice; JetBrains Mono is the machine voice. Metrics, labels, paths, status lines, form fields are machine, so mono. Headings, sentences, names are human, so Inter.

rules.name: The Quiet-Glow Heading Rule
rules.body: Headings are solid Instrument White (#EDEFF2). Gradient text is forbidden as decoration; the only exception is a restrained vertical metallic sheen on the hero name, AA-tested, never multi-hue.

rules.name: The Glow-Belongs-To-The-Machine Rule
rules.body: Glow is reserved for the network and the final output node. If a button, card, or input glows, it is impersonating the machine; remove it.

dos: Do keep the background absolute charcoal (#05070C) and build depth with tonal lift to #0B0F17 plus hairline borders, never drop shadows.
Do reserve Electric Lime (#CCFF00) for the active/live signal only and keep it rare.
Do use cobalt (#3B82F6 / #1E40AF) for everything structural: synapses, outlines, links, resting network.
Do set machine-spoken strings in JetBrains Mono and human-spoken text in Inter.
Do keep body text in Instrument White (#EDEFF2) and verify every text color clears WCAG AA (4.5:1); placeholders use Muted Ink, not faint gray.
Do confine glow to the canvas network and the output node.
Do provide the prefers-reduced-motion static-network fallback and the <768px stacked-layout fallback; both are mandatory.
donts: Don't use gradient text except the one permitted restrained metallic sheen on the hero name; no multi-hue gradient text.
Don't ship the generic SaaS landing: no gradient-blob hero, no big-number hero-metric template, no identical icon-heading-text card grid.
Don't ship the cluttered dev portfolio: no skill-cloud walls, no badge dumps, no busy-resume density.
Don't ship the corporate / stiff look: no enterprise-blue chrome, no stock photography, no personality-free polish.
Don't use cobalt for reading-length body text, and never use Deep Cobalt (#1E40AF) for any text.
Don't put a colored border-left/border-right stripe on cards or callouts; use full hairline borders.
Don't glow UI chrome (buttons, cards, inputs). Glow belongs to the machine.
Don't render network nodes or synapses as DOM/SVG elements; everything inside the network is painted on the single canvas.
Don't use lime for error states; errors get a dedicated dim red-orange.
